title: Development and validation of RT-PCR and nested PCR assays for detection of Sabiá virus (SABV)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b3juh","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"85unb","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"i66c","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"notes","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":"This protocol describes the design, validation, and application of conventional RT-PCR and nested PCR assays for detection of Sabiá virus (SABV). Primer sets were designed based on the 1990 reference genome and newly generated SABV genomes, targeting conserved regions of the nucleoprotein gene. The protocol includes RT-PCR screening and nested PCR amplification optimized for low-viremia samples."}}}
steps: ProtoScript II First Strand cDNA Synthesis Kit (New England Biolabs)

Outer PCR Combine the following per reaction to create the amplification master mix: %s %s Incubate on thermocycler as follows: %s %s Inner PCR
Degenerate bases were incorporated in selected primers to accommodate sequence variability.
Shorter amplicons were prioritized for degraded RNA.
Nested PCR should be handled carefully to avoid cross-contamination.
creator: Ingra Morales
